title: Revert Pod LTX transformer GGUF -> bf16 (cu130 killed the cold tax that justified GGUF)
description:
FOLLOW-UP to MPI-189 (blocked on it). The Q8_0 GGUF LTX transformer on the Pod (MPI-168, engines.remote.extraDeps in models.js) existed for ONE reason: to sidestep the ~5min aimdo cold tax on the old cu126/cu128 stack (bf16 41GB faulted in 108-127s per stage). cu130 (MPI-187) collapses that fault-in to ~11s -> the GGUF's whole justification is gone.

=== WHY REVERT TO bf16 ON THE POD ===
1. QUALITY: bf16 is the reference; GGUF Q8 is a quality trade. Local already runs bf16 (engines.local.extraDeps).
2. KILLS MPI-185 ROOT CAUSE: the LTX i2v OOM on 24GB is the ComfyUI-GGUF dequantize_blocks_BF16 upcast spike (int16->int32->float32) at sample time. No GGUF = no dequant = no spike = OOM gone. (bf16 still needs a 32GB card, but that's a clean VRAM fit, not a transient dequant explosion.)
3. SIMPLER: drops the engine-split on the transformer — both engines run the same bf16 file. Removes ComfyUI-GGUF node from the remote deps, the _gguf workflow suffix, and the GGUF gguf-loader path. One transformer, one workflow set. Big consumer-sweep reduction (the MPI-163/165 engine-split smear shrinks).

=== SCOPE ===
- models.js ltx-23 engines block: remote.extraDeps ['ltx23-transformer-gguf','ComfyUI-GGUF'] -> ['ltx23-transformer-bf16'] (= same as local); drop workflowSuffix '_gguf' on remote (both engines -> the bf16 workflow files). Possibly collapse the engines block entirely if local==remote now.
- Verify the LTX workflow set: the _gguf.json variants become unused -> remove or regenerate. The bf16 LTX_{t2v,i2v}{,_stage2}.json become the single set.
- dependencies.js: ltx23-transformer-gguf + ComfyUI-GGUF may become orphaned (check no other model uses them before removing).
- Sweep ALL engine-split consumers (download / status-gate / prompt-box / resolveModelDeps) per the engine-split law — a half-revert leaves a broken resolver. cf .claude/rules/comfy_engine.md Engine Split.

=== GATE ===
Do NOT start until MPI-189 ships + a cu130 Pod live-confirms bf16 LTX faults in ~11s AND fits (32GB card, no OOM through both stages). If cu130-bf16 still OOMs the continue stage on 32GB, keep GGUF as a fallback for smaller cards and only revert the >=32GB tier. Verify on a real cu130 Pod before ripping GGUF out. This card also finally CLOSES MPI-185 (the OOM investigation) once bf16 is confirmed clean on cu130.

BRANCH: RunPod. Cross-ref: MPI-189 (prereq), MPI-187 (cu130 proof), MPI-185 (OOM this closes), MPI-168 (the GGUF-add being reverted), MPI-163/165 (engine-split to unwind).
